nodes.label: AI as learning partner for understanding
nodes.grounding_span: my main goal is to understand how to do it myself, and AI tools are invaluable to me in these types of explanations

nodes.label: AI for simple, verifiable tasks
nodes.grounding_span: The tasks I trust AI to complete alone are typically very simple in nature

nodes.label: AI code ownership and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: code I don't understand or code I haven't written myself is not very useful in the long term when it comes time to debug

nodes.label: AI surprise with working solution
nodes.grounding_span: the most satisfying results achieved from AI are those that surprise me with a working solution out of the box

nodes.label: AI reliance on up-to-date information
nodes.grounding_span: if the models were able to better use online information (like up-to-date documentation) they read about and successfully tie their learnings in without over-focusing on examples

nodes.label: Deep personal understanding and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: my main goal is to understand how to do it myself

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: any typing I can save is optimal

nodes.label: Control and ownership of code
nodes.grounding_span: I also like to have complete control and understanding of my code

nodes.label: Enjoyment and fun in programming
nodes.grounding_span: I do have fun programming in my work

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as learning partner
nodes.grounding_span: AI tools are invaluable to me in these types of explanations

nodes.label: Frustration with AI missing simple issues
nodes.grounding_span: A super trivial solution that was overlooked by each AI model I asked

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI surprising solution
nodes.grounding_span: the most satisfying results achieved from AI are those that surprise me with a working solution out of the box

nodes.label: Openness to more AI use if capabilities improve
nodes.grounding_span: I'd be more inclined to ask more questions and use their code examples without feeling like I need to go learn more about it myself

nodes.label: Iterative back-and-forth questioning for deep understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I have a back-and-forth conversation and ask the AI to provide usage examples, then decide how I can implement those examples

nodes.label: Deliberate boundary-setting between AI and personal work
nodes.grounding_span: I typically don't let the AI handle the entire task for me
